2604.16235.title: Optimizing Korean-Centric LLMs via Token Pruning
2604.16235.paper_summary: This paper presents a systematic benchmark of state-of-the-art multilingual large language models (LLMs) adapted via token pruning - a compression technique that eliminates tokens and embedding parameters corresponding to languages irrelevant to the target application. Focusing on Korean-centric natural language processing (NLP) tasks, we evaluate architectures including Qwen3, Gemma-3, Llama-3, and Aya across three vocabulary configurations: Original, English-Korean (EnKo), and English-Korean-Chinese (EnKoZh). Performance is assessed using established benchmarks for general aptitude, cultural literacy, instruction following, and machine translation. Our findings indicate that token pruning significantly improves generation stability by eliminating language confusion, and in the case of machine translation, frequently enhances performance on Korean-specific tasks. While instruction-following capabilities display architecture-dependent variance linked to latent cross-lingual representations, the significant reduction in vocabulary size validates token pruning as a highly effective optimization strategy for memory-constrained, domain-specific deployments, despite modest gains in inference latency.
2604.16235.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文呈現了一個系統性的基準測試，針對通過標記修剪調整的最先進多語言大型語言模型（LLMs）進行評估——這是一種壓縮技術，旨在消除與目標應用無關的語言所對應的標記和嵌入參數。<lb/>專注於以韓語為中心的自然語言處理（NLP）任務，我們評估了包括Qwen3、Gemma-3、Llama-3和Aya在內的架構，並針對三種詞彙配置進行評估：原始、英韓（EnKo）和英韓中文（EnKoZh）。<lb/>性能評估使用了針對一般能力、文化素養、指令跟隨和機器翻譯的既定基準。<lb/>我們的研究結果表明，標記修剪通過消除語言混淆顯著提高了生成的穩定性，並且在機器翻譯的情況下，經常增強了在韓語特定任務上的表現。<lb/>雖然指令跟隨能力顯示出與潛在跨語言表徵相關的架構依賴性變異，但詞彙大小的顯著減少驗證了標記修剪作為一種對於內存受限、特定領域部署的高度有效優化策略，儘管推理延遲的增益較為有限。
2604.16235.author: Hoyeol Kim et.al.
